stories.teaser: A garden designer living with muscular dystrophy says recent changes to his NDIS plan put his care at risk.
stories.full_content:
Joshua Ruff, a central Victorian garden designer featured on the ABC's Gardening Australia, lives with Duchenne muscular dystrophy and relies on NDIS support for his daily care. He has raised concerns about recent changes to his funding plan, saying they leave him without adequate assistance for essential activities.

The changes came without sufficient warning, and Ruff says the new plan does not account for the level of support his condition requires. His case illustrates broader concerns about how NDIS plan reviews are affecting participants who depend on consistent, reliable care arrangements.

Ruff's experience highlights the importance of stability in disability support funding, particularly for people managing progressive conditions that require ongoing, tailored assistance.

stories.teaser: The mountain village of Paluma, north of Townsville, is welcoming visitors again after road access was restored.
stories.full_content:
Paluma, a rainforest village in the mountains north of Townsville, has reopened to visitors 16 months after a major deluge destroyed the main road access. The restoration of the road connection allows locals and visitors to return to the area and resume normal community and business activities.

The extended closure had cut the village off from easy access and affected the local economy. Road repairs took considerable time, but infrastructure work has now progressed enough to safely restore public access through the area.

The reopening brings relief to residents and business owners who depend on visitor numbers. It also marks a recovery milestone for the broader Townsville region following the flooding event.

stories.teaser: Two people were injured during a police pursuit in Perth's north-east, and Transperth is managing technical delays on the transport network.
stories.full_content:
Emergency services responded to a police chase in Perth's north-east that resulted in vehicle damage and injuries to two individuals. The incident highlights the ongoing traffic and safety challenges that occur when pursuit situations develop in built-up areas.

Separately, the Transperth transport system reported technical difficulties affecting commuter services. Both situations caused disruptions to Perth residents' daily routines during the morning period.

Authorities are managing the aftermath of both incidents, with transport services working to restore normal operations.

stories.teaser: France is recording significantly elevated daily death rates during an extreme heatwave affecting more than 150 million Europeans.
stories.full_content:
Europe is currently experiencing record-level temperatures, with scientists describing it as the most intense heatwave the continent has recorded in its measurement history. France alone is documenting around 1,000 excess daily deaths attributed to the prolonged heat, indicating a substantial public health impact.

The extreme temperatures have affected more than 150 million people across Europe. Such widespread heat events carry particular risk for older people, those with existing health conditions, and others unable to access adequate cooling.

This event underscores the increasing intensity of temperature extremes in Europe and the importance of public health preparedness for extreme weather.

stories.teaser: More than 5,000 New Zealand secondary students have failed the NCEA numeracy assessment four or more times.
stories.full_content:
Data from New Zealand's education system shows that over 5,000 teenagers who met literacy and numeracy requirements in the previous year resorted to alternative assessment standards rather than passing the standard numeracy test. This represents approximately 14 percent of students completing NCEA numeracy requirements.

The reliance on alternative pathways suggests that traditional numeracy assessment may not be suitable for all learners, or that some students require additional support to meet standard benchmarks. Schools have the flexibility to offer alternative assessment methods when students struggle with repeated attempts.

The figures highlight ongoing discussions about how numeracy education is assessed and whether current standards are reaching all students effectively.

stories.teaser: Cameron Myers and Sarah Billings set national track records at the Paris Diamond League event.
stories.full_content:
Australian track stars Cameron Myers and Sarah Billings both achieved national record performances at the Diamond League meet in Paris. Myers won the 1,500 metres final with a time that eclipsed the previous Australian record, while Billings, an 800 metres specialist, also established a new national mark at the event.

The performances reflect the depth of talent in Australian middle-distance running. Both athletes have been developing consistently and their records demonstrate the current strength of the country's track program at elite competition level.

These achievements at a major international meet signal encouraging momentum heading into future competitions.

stories.teaser: A daughter has declined a compensation settlement from a retirement village operator so she could speak publicly about her mother's injuries.
stories.full_content:
Nicky Foreman chose to forego a confidential compensation agreement with Ryman, the operator of the retirement village where her mother was injured, in order to speak openly about what happened. The decision to step outside a settlement agreement reflects her determination to make the incident public rather than accept a private financial resolution.

Foreman's mother sustained serious injuries at the facility, and the daughter felt it was important to detail her family's experience. By declining the settlement payout, she preserved her ability to discuss the case without legal restrictions that typically accompany compensation agreements.

The case raises questions about the transparency and accountability of retirement village operators when incidents occur, and how settlement terms can limit public discussion of safety issues.

stories.headline: No one is self-made
stories.summary: The idea that success is deserved has great traction in the world. But Zhuangzi argues that it is a deeply flawed notion

stories.headline: The New Seismic Discovery Beneath the Surface of Mars
stories.summary: Earth isn't alone—in its rock recycling processes
